[A] A bi-objective MECS-to-controller assignment minimizing controller response time and PACKET_IN transmission overhead, using M/G/1 sojourn and hop-weighted request load models, solved under capacity and limited-migration constraints via a decomposition-based evolutionary algorithm with coordinate-transformation selection [B] A single-objective assignment minimizing only PACKET_IN overhead with M/M/1 modeling, solved by simulated annealing without capacity or migration constraints [C] A bi-objective problem minimizing response time and link utilization using G/G/1 modeling and exact integer linear programming without migration limits [D] A response-time-only objective with M/D/1 modeling, greedy selection, and unrestricted migrations regardless of hop distances

In SDN-enabled MEC with multiple controllers, modeling controller response time as M/G/1 sojourn and hop-weighted propagation/transmission overhead, and solving the resulting bi-objective MECS-to-controller assignment via a _________(decomposition-based evolutionary algorithm/greedy heuristic) with coordinate-transformation selection, jointly reduces control-plane response time and data-plane PACKET_IN transmission overhead while balancing controller load under capacity and limited-migration constraints.

Assertion (A): In SDN-enabled MEC with multiple controllers, modeling controller response time as M/G/1 sojourn and propagation/transmission overhead as hop-weighted request load, and solving the bi-objective MECS-to-controller assignment via a decomposition-based evolutionary algorithm with coordinate-transformation selection, jointly reduces control-plane response time and data-plane PACKET_IN transmission overhead while balancing controller load under capacity and limited-migration constraints. Reason (R): This outcome occurs because the formulation ignores hop distances and omits capacity and migration constraints.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): When the maximum number of concurrent MECS redistributions per step is capped, which statement best characterizes the resulting search space, controller resource behavior, and convergence outcome? [A] Search space grows, the controller experiences transient resource spikes, and convergence to high-quality assignments requires indefinite time. [B] Search space shrinks, the controller avoids transient resource spikes, and convergence to high-quality assignments occurs within effective time. [C] Search space is unchanged, the controller sees permanent resource spikes, and convergence fails within any reasonable time. [D] Search space shrinks, the controller endures transient resource spikes, and convergence is only to low-quality assignments beyond effective time.

Capping the maximum number of concurrent MECS redistributions per step increases the search space and exacerbates transient controller resource spikes, preventing convergence to high-quality assignments within effective time.

Capping the maximum number of concurrent MECS redistributions per step reduces search space and _________(prevents/triggers) transient controller resource spikes, enabling convergence to high-quality assignments within effective time.

Assertion (A): Capping the maximum number of concurrent MECS redistributions per step reduces search space and prevents transient controller resource spikes, enabling convergence to high-quality assignments within effective time. Reason (R): Capping the number of concurrent MECS redistributions increases the search space and causes transient controller resource spikes.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Select the accurate description of how adding controllers influences delay and overhead and what happens after the saturation threshold, taking into account topology/load dependence and the risk in heterogeneous (scale-free) networks if topology is ignored. [A] Benefits scale linearly with controller count, and overhead reduces while delay may increase, independent of topology and load. [B] Benefits taper at a saturation that depends on topology and load; beyond it, extra controllers offer no benefit and can harm performance in heterogeneous (scale-free) networks when topology is not considered. [C] The saturation threshold is the same across topologies and loads, and extra controllers always help in heterogeneous (scale-free) networks. [D] Saturation occurs only under extreme load and in homogeneous networks; heterogeneous networks are unaffected by topology-agnostic controller additions.

Adding controllers monotonically reduces average response delay and transmission overhead with a topology- and load-independent saturation, and additional controllers never degrade performance in heterogeneous (scale-free) networks even if topology is ignored.

Assertion (A): Increasing the number of controllers always reduces average response delay and transmission overhead, with no saturation or risk of degradation in heterogeneous (scale-free) networks. Reason (R): The saturation point for benefits from additional controllers is dependent on topology and load.
output: A is False, R is True

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which combination of model inputs, path coverage, executability mechanism, and suite curation aligns with an approach that automatically derives executable end-to-end test scripts for IoT systems? [A] UML state machine only; all-states coverage; random search for executability; retain redundant tests to maximize count. [B] UML class diagrams only; boundary-based path selection; symbolic execution for feasibility; no redundancy minimization. [C] UML state machine and class diagrams; all-transitions-covering paths; branch-distance-guided executability heuristic; redundancy minimized in the suite. [D] UML activity and deployment diagrams; pairwise transition coverage; SAT-based feasibility; prioritize rare transitions without removing redundancy.

A model-based approach uses UML activity diagrams of an IoT system to automatically derive exploratory test cases by generating all-states-covering paths without executability guidance and by retaining redundant scripts in the resulting suite.

A model-based approach uses UML state machine and class diagrams of an IoT system to automatically derive executable end-to-end test scripts by generating all-transitions-covering test paths, making them feasible with a _________(branch-distance-guided/random) executability heuristic, and minimizing redundancy in the resulting suite.

Assertion (A): A model-based approach uses UML state machine and class diagrams of an IoT system to automatically derive executable end-to-end test scripts by generating all-transitions-covering test paths, making them feasible with a branch-distance-guided executability heuristic, and minimizing redundancy in the resulting suite. Reason (R): Feasibility is guaranteed because UML class diagrams encode executable code for each transition, making branch-distance guidance unnecessary.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Select the statement that completely describes how the test scripts handle both the UI and sensors/actuators. [A] They drive the UI with custom record-and-playback tools and capture sensor data only from real hardware [B] They bypass the UI by invoking internal APIs and emulate actuators with stubs [C] They drive the UI using standard automation frameworks and both stimulate and observe sensors and actuators via virtualized components or adapters for physical devices [D] They only validate UI rendering and do not interact with sensors or actuators

Assertion (A): The produced test scripts exercise the system via the user interface using standard automation frameworks while stimulating and observing sensors and actuators through virtualized components or adapters for physical devices. Reason (R): They achieve this by bypassing the user interface and relying solely on direct API calls to the system.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which combination correctly states the incorporation method for arbitrary environmental inputs, the model encoding, and the intended purpose? [A] Boundary value analysis; partitions as a single transition; automated parameter tuning. [B] Clustering; partitions as shared parameters; manual path authoring. [C] Equivalence partitioning; each partition as a distinct operation; automated path generation. [D] Random fuzzing; partitions as stochastic nodes; interactive path labeling.

Assertion (A): Arbitrary environmental inputs are incorporated by equivalence partitioning, with each partition encoded as a distinct operation in the model to enable automated path generation. Reason (R): Encoding all partitions as a single operation yields the same automation benefits with less modeling effort.
output: A is True, R is False

input: On representative IoT systems under mutation testing, the generated test suites effectively detect faults, and the suite minimization step reduces execution time _________(without compromising fault detection/while degrading fault detection).

Assertion (A): On representative IoT systems evaluated with mutation testing, generated test suites effectively detect faults, and suite minimization reduces execution time without compromising fault detection capability. Reason (R): Suite minimization reduces execution time by discarding fault-detecting tests, which necessarily lowers fault detection capability.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Identify the approach that satisfies all of the following for few-shot image classification: objects are composed from two shared dictionaries (component embeddings and prototypical component spatial activation patterns), the dictionaries are learned end-to-end under clustering and sparsity regularization, an attention mechanism reweights class-discriminative components, and efficient transfer to novel classes arises from recombining the reusable elements. [A] Class-specific templates trained without clustering or sparsity, with attention suppressing discriminative components and transfer via full fine-tuning. [B] A single shared dictionary of spatial activation patterns only, with attention reweighting background components, and transfer not achieved through recombination. [C] Two shared dictionaries—component embeddings and prototypical component spatial activation patterns—learned end-to-end with clustering and sparsity, with attention reweighting class-discriminative components to enable recombination-based transfer to novel classes. [D] Many-shot image classification using per-class embeddings not learned end-to-end, without clustering or sparsity and without attention, with transfer requiring retraining rather than recombination.

Few-shot image classification requires per-class, non-shared component dictionaries and does not benefit from attention-based reweighting, so transfer to novel classes depends on full fine-tuning rather than recombining learned elements.

A shared dictionary of _________(prototypical component spatial activation patterns/global image prototypes) is paired with a shared dictionary of component embeddings to represent objects for few-shot image classification, learned end-to-end with clustering and sparsity regularization and modulated by attention that reweights class-discriminative components, enabling efficient transfer to novel classes by recombining reusable elements.

Assertion (A): Few-shot image classification can be performed by representing objects as compositions of two shared dictionaries—component embeddings and prototypical component spatial activation patterns—learned end-to-end with clustering and sparsity, with attention reweighting class-discriminative components to enable recombination-based transfer to novel classes. Reason (R): Transfer to novel classes in this setting depends primarily on increasing the number of labeled examples rather than recombining learned components.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): In a meta-testing scenario for recognizing unseen classes, which statement correctly specifies the data representation and trainable versus fixed components? [A] Use learned compositional features while jointly updating the backbone and dictionaries [B] Train only a simple classifier on the learned compositional features and keep the backbone and dictionaries fixed [C] Freeze the features and dictionaries but fine-tune the backbone [D] Retrain all network components because unseen classes cannot be recognized otherwise
During meta-testing, recognizing unseen classes requires updating the backbone or dictionaries in addition to training a classifier on the learned compositional features.
During meta-testing, unseen classes can be recognized by training only a simple classifier on learned compositional features while the backbone and dictionaries are kept _________(fixed/trainable).

Assertion (A): During meta-testing, unseen classes can be recognized by training only a simple classifier on the learned compositional features while keeping the backbone and dictionaries fixed. Reason (R): Recognizing unseen classes during meta-testing necessitates end-to-end fine-tuning of the backbone and dictionaries.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement best characterizes distortion-aware K-means clustering for RF energy-harvesting wireless sensor networks that exhibit spatially correlated observations? [A] It operates in networks with independent observations and uses MAP-based multi-hop models to make all nodes relays, ignoring reconstruction distortion. [B] It targets battery-powered networks and uses only single-hop MMSE to make every node a reporting node without role differentiation or distortion guarantees. [C] It operates in RF energy-harvesting networks with spatially correlated observations by using MMSE-based single-hop and two-hop distortion models to select only essential reporting nodes and designate the remaining nodes as relay heads so that a specified reconstruction distortion is met. [D] It uses standard K-means without distortion awareness to randomly alternate node roles for energy balancing, with no target on reconstruction distortion.

In RF energy-harvesting wireless sensor networks with spatially correlated observations, distortion-aware K-means clustering selects all nodes as reporting nodes using only single-hop models and does not enforce any target reconstruction distortion.

Assertion (A): In RF energy-harvesting wireless sensor networks with spatially correlated observations, distortion-aware K-means clustering meets a target reconstruction distortion by choosing only essential reporting nodes and making the rest relay heads based on MMSE-based single-hop and two-hop distortion models. Reason (R): The approach prioritizes maximizing throughput over distortion control, so it does not enforce a reconstruction distortion target.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): When comparing two-hop clustered relaying with single-hop direct transmission, which combination of outcomes is accurate regarding energy use, network lifetime, and reconstruction distortion, and what accounts for the distortion? [A] Lower energy use and longer network lifetime, with higher reconstruction distortion caused by the additional relay channel noise. [B] Higher energy use and shorter network lifetime, with higher reconstruction distortion caused by multipath fading alone. [C] Lower energy use and longer network lifetime, with lower reconstruction distortion because the relay suppresses noise. [D] No change in energy use or lifetime, with higher reconstruction distortion due to congestion at the relay.

Compared with single-hop direct transmission, two-hop clustered relaying reduces energy consumption and extends network lifetime at the cost of _________(higher/lower) reconstruction distortion due to the additional relay channel noise.

Assertion (A): Two-hop clustered relaying reduces energy consumption and extends network lifetime compared with single-hop direct transmission while incurring higher reconstruction distortion. Reason (R): This occurs because the relay eliminates second-hop noise, thereby lowering reconstruction distortion.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Within a distortion-aware clustering framework, exploiting ambient RF energy harvesting changes neither the number of alive nodes nor the operation duration as harvested power increases unless distortion constraints are violated.

Within a distortion-aware clustering framework, exploiting _________(ambient RF energy harvesting/solar energy harvesting) increases the number of alive nodes and prolongs operation duration as harvested power increases without violating distortion constraints.

Assertion (A): Within a distortion-aware clustering framework, exploiting ambient RF energy harvesting increases the number of alive nodes and prolongs operation duration as harvested power increases without violating distortion constraints. Reason (R): Higher harvested RF power requires relaxing distortion constraints to achieve longer operation and more alive nodes.
output: A is True, R is False

input: A binary multilayer perceptron using eight histogram-derived features—kurtosis, skewness, standard deviation, range, median, geometric median, Hjorth mobility, and Hjorth complexity—reliably distinguishes _________(LSB-steganographed/transform-steganographed) from cover monochromatic 256x256 8-bit images across payloads 0.1–0.5 bpp, with classification performance improving as payload increases.

Assertion (A): A binary multilayer perceptron using the specified eight histogram-derived features reliably distinguishes LSB-steganographed from cover monochromatic 256x256 8-bit images across payloads 0.1–0.5 bpp, and classification performance improves as payload increases. Reason (R): The images are color 24-bit, so the classifier benefits from chromatic information.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Under an LSB embedding scheme that chooses modified pixels using a pseudo-random sequence and embeds by XORing carrier least significant bits with message bits, which relationship best describes PSNR versus payload over the examined range? [A] PSNR follows an exponential decay with payload. [B] PSNR exhibits a quadratic dependence with a minimum at mid-range payloads. [C] PSNR declines roughly linearly as payload increases. [D] PSNR improves due to averaging effects as payload increases.

In LSB embedding with pseudo-random pixel selection and XOR embedding between carrier least significant bits and message bits, the PSNR of the stego image increases approximately linearly with increasing payload over the examined range.

In the examined setting, modified pixels are selected via a _________(pseudo-random sequence/deterministic raster scan) and XOR is applied between carrier least significant bits and message bits, resulting in PSNR that decreases approximately linearly with increasing payload.

Assertion (A): For LSB embedding with pseudo-random selection of modified pixels and XOR between carrier least significant bits and message bits, the PSNR of the stego image decreases approximately linearly as payload increases over the examined range. Reason (R): The selection of modified pixels is deterministic and no LSBs are altered when embedding, so payload does not affect PSNR.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which description aligns with a sentient multimedia architecture that enables real-time workplace safety by combining edge machine-learning wearables, fixed area devices, and a cloud-based safety coordination platform to fuse multi-source sensor data, classify hazardous events such as falls from accelerometer and gyroscope signals using low-pass filtering with statistical features, and issue coordinated alarms and guidance via predictive and prescriptive analytics? [A] Edge wearables plus fixed area devices with cloud coordination, multi-sensor fusion, low-pass filtering with statistical features on accelerometer and gyroscope data to classify hazardous events, and predictive and prescriptive analytics for coordinated alarms and guidance in real time. [B] Edge wearables with cloud support only, independent per-sensor processing using band-pass filtering without statistical features, and uncoordinated alerts without prescriptive analytics. [C] Fixed area devices without edge wearables, high-pass filtering with heuristic rules to identify general motion patterns, and periodic summaries instead of real-time guidance. [D] All three tiers present but no data fusion, omission of hazardous event classification, and exclusive use of descriptive analytics to provide dashboards without coordinated alarms.

An integrated sentient multimedia architecture that combines edge wearables, fixed area devices, and a cloud platform enables offline workplace safety by processing single-source data without fusion, identifying general activities using high-pass filtering without statistical features, and sending uncoordinated alerts without predictive or prescriptive analytics.

To enable real-time workplace safety, an integrated sentient multimedia architecture that combines edge machine-learning wearables, fixed area devices, and a cloud-based safety coordination platform fuses multi-source sensor data, classifies hazardous events such as falls from accelerometer and gyroscope signals with low-pass filtering and statistical features, and issues coordinated alarms and guidance through _________(predictive and prescriptive/descriptive and diagnostic) analytics.

Assertion (A): Real-time workplace safety is enabled by an integrated sentient multimedia architecture that combines edge machine-learning wearables, fixed area devices, and a cloud-based safety coordination platform, fuses multi-source sensor data, classifies hazardous events such as falls from accelerometer and gyroscope signals using low-pass filtering with statistical features, and issues coordinated alarms and guidance through predictive and prescriptive analytics. Reason (R): High-pass filtering without statistical features is sufficient for hazardous event classification from accelerometer and gyroscope signals in this context.
output: A is True, R is False

input: In perceptual decision-making with accuracy feedback, prior beliefs do not bias reliance on agent judgments; when estimate distributions are identical, humans initially exert stronger influence than social robots or computers, and this bias grows with repeated exposure.

In a no-feedback perceptual decision context where estimate distributions from social robots, humans, and computers are identical, prior-belief-driven reliance on judgments favors social robots initially over the other agents, and this bias _________(weakens/strengthens) with repeated exposure to the partner’s responses.

Assertion (A): In perceptual decision-making without accuracy feedback, prior beliefs about an agent’s nature bias reliance on its judgments, giving social robots initially stronger influence than humans or computers despite identical estimate distributions, and the bias weakens with repeated exposure to the partner’s responses. Reason (R): Social robots provide systematically more accurate estimates than humans or computers, which amplifies the bias over time.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Identify the statement that accurately captures how susceptibility to influence is shaped across interaction types when overt socially relevant signals are absent. [A] In human-human and human-robot interaction, susceptibility is governed by the same mechanism: social normative dynamics. [B] In human-human interaction, susceptibility is shaped by social normative dynamics beyond perceived competence, whereas in human-robot interaction it is governed primarily by informational assessments of the robot's competence. [C] In human-human interaction, susceptibility is governed primarily by informational assessments of the partner's competence, while in human-robot interaction it is driven by social normative dynamics beyond competence. [D] In both human-human and human-robot interaction, susceptibility is governed primarily by informational assessments of competence.

Assertion (A): When overt socially relevant signals are absent, susceptibility to influence differs by interaction type: in human-human interaction it is shaped by social normative dynamics beyond perceived competence, whereas in human-robot interaction it is governed primarily by informational assessments of the robot's competence. Reason (R): In both interaction types, susceptibility to influence is governed primarily by perceived competence rather than social normative dynamics.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Identify the configuration that, compared to using CNN features alone, yields improved voxel-wise brain tumor tissue segmentation accuracy and boundary delineation in multi-modal MRI while keeping model complexity low. [A] CNN-derived features from a transfer-learned CIFAR architecture concatenated with a per-voxel histogram-based texture descriptor encoding local neighborhood dependencies and normalized intensities, followed by decision tree classification. [B] CNN-derived features alone from a transfer-learned CIFAR architecture fed into a deep neural classifier with many parameters. [C] CNN-derived features combined with a per-voxel histogram descriptor that ignores neighborhood information, followed by k-nearest neighbors. [D] Handcrafted edge features alone classified by a random forest in single-modality MRI.

Augmenting transfer-learned CIFAR CNN features with a histogram descriptor and classifying with a decision tree yields no improvement in voxel-wise brain tumor tissue segmentation accuracy or boundary delineation over CNN features alone and increases model complexity in multi-modal MRI.

Relative to using only CNN features, pairing transfer-learned CIFAR CNN features with a per-voxel histogram-based texture descriptor that encodes local neighborhood dependencies and normalized intensities and classifying with a decision tree improves voxel-wise brain tumor tissue segmentation accuracy and boundary delineation in multi-modal MRI while maintaining _________(low/high) model complexity.

Assertion (A): Combining transfer-learned CIFAR CNN features with a per-voxel histogram-based texture descriptor encoding local neighborhood dependencies and normalized intensities, followed by decision tree classification, improves voxel-wise brain tumor tissue segmentation accuracy and boundary delineation in multi-modal MRI compared to using CNN features alone while maintaining low model complexity. Reason (R): The observed improvement is attributable to higher model complexity rather than complementary texture information.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which fine-tuning data sources are associated with better performance in cited text span identification relative to using generic pre-trained encoders without domain adaptation? [A] Random out-of-domain sentences from unrelated corpora [B] In-domain unlabelled text or augmented citing–cited sentence pairs [C] Supervised labels from an unrelated classification task [D] No fine-tuning data, leaving the encoders generic

For cited text span identification, domain adaptation by fine-tuning on in-domain unlabelled text or augmented citing–cited sentence pairs does not improve performance over generic pre-trained encoders without domain adaptation.

Assertion (A): Fine-tuning on in-domain unlabelled text or augmented citing–cited sentence pairs enhances cited text span identification relative to generic pre-trained encoders without domain adaptation. Reason (R): Augmented citing–cited sentence pairs inherently provide gold-standard span annotations for direct supervised training.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Select the correct effect of supplying only the abstract and identified cited text spans of a reference article to a BERT-based extractive summariser, relative to supplying the full text. [A] It typically yields lower-quality summaries and increases input length. [B] It can match or surpass summary quality while reducing input length. [C] It requires post-processing to avoid quality loss and keeps input length unchanged. [D] It improves quality only when the input is the full text, not when restricted to abstracts and cited spans.

Assertion (A): Providing only the abstract and identified cited text spans of a reference article as input to a BERT-based extractive summariser can match or surpass the summary quality obtained from the full text while reducing input length. Reason (R): Using only the abstract and identified cited text spans increases input length compared to the full text.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which conditioning scheme ensures that a hierarchical Vecchia approximation yields a Gaussian-process model whose covariance and precision matrices have sparse Cholesky factors with the same sparsity pattern, and with the precision factor equal to the inverse transpose of the covariance factor? [A] Conditioning each hierarchical group on its nested ancestors [B] Conditioning each group on non-nested descendants [C] Conditioning each group on all other groups simultaneously [D] No conditioning between hierarchical groups

Under a hierarchical Vecchia approximation where each group conditions on its nested ancestors, the precision Cholesky factor equals the _________(inverse transpose/transpose) of the covariance Cholesky factor, with both factors sparse and sharing the same sparsity pattern.

Assertion (A): A hierarchical Vecchia approximation that conditions each hierarchical group on its nested ancestors yields a Gaussian-process model whose covariance and precision matrices have sparse Cholesky factors with the same sparsity pattern, and the precision factor equals the inverse transpose of the covariance factor. Reason (R): These properties hold because any covariance matrix admits a Cholesky factor that is always dense regardless of the approximation or conditioning.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Under independent Gaussian observation noise, the posterior covariance and precision Cholesky factors induced by the hierarchical Vecchia prior become strictly denser than the prior factors.

The posterior covariance and precision Cholesky factors induced by the hierarchical Vecchia prior retain exactly the same sparsity patterns as the prior factors when the observation noise is _________(independent Gaussian/dependent Gaussian).

Assertion (A): Under independent Gaussian observation noise, the posterior covariance and precision Cholesky factors induced by the hierarchical Vecchia prior retain exactly the same sparsity patterns as the prior factors. Reason (R): The observation noise is dependent and non-Gaussian.
output: A is True, R is False

input: The covariance Cholesky factor implied by the hierarchical Vecchia approximation equals the incomplete Cholesky factorization of the exact covariance without any sparsity restriction and yields O(n N) time and O(n) memory regardless of conditioning set size.

Given that the covariance Cholesky factor implied by the hierarchical Vecchia approximation equals the incomplete Cholesky factorization of the exact covariance restricted to the specified sparsity pattern, inference scales as _________(O(n N^2)/O(n^2 N)) time and O(n N) memory when conditioning sets have size at most N.

Assertion (A): The covariance Cholesky factor implied by the hierarchical Vecchia approximation equals the incomplete Cholesky factorization of the exact covariance restricted to the specified sparsity pattern, enabling inference that scales as O(n N^2) time and O(n N) memory when conditioning sets have size at most N. Reason (R): This holds because the factorization is performed without enforcing any sparsity pattern and uses conditioning sets of size n.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement correctly characterizes the result of combining a hierarchical Vecchia structure with a Laplace approximation for exponential-family likelihoods? [A] It yields a stochastic-gradient method that needs no concavity and targets a heavy-tailed posterior [B] It yields a conjugate-gradient method that preserves sparsity but converges to a uniform prior approximation [C] It yields a Newton–Raphson-based algorithm that preserves the same sparsity and, under concavity, converges to a Gaussian approximation at the posterior mode [D] It yields a coordinate-descent algorithm that densifies computations and converges to a point estimate without distributional approximation

Under concavity, the resulting Newton–Raphson-based algorithm from combining a hierarchical Vecchia structure with a Laplace approximation for exponential-family likelihoods converges to a _________(Gaussian/nonparametric) approximation at the posterior mode while exploiting the same sparsity.

Assertion (A): Combining a hierarchical Vecchia structure with a Laplace approximation for exponential-family likelihoods yields a Newton–Raphson-based algorithm that preserves the same sparsity and converges under concavity to a Gaussian approximation at the posterior mode. Reason (R): Stochastic gradient methods guarantee convergence to non-Gaussian posteriors without any concavity requirements.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement correctly characterizes the outcome of embedding the hierarchical Vecchia structure into linear and linearized spatiotemporal state-space models? [A] It yields Kalman–Vecchia–Laplace filters that break sparsity in updates and scale linearly only when temporal evolution is dense [B] It yields Kalman–Vecchia–Laplace filters that preserve sparsity through forecast and update and scale approximately linearly with state dimension when temporal evolution is sparse [C] It yields non-Kalman–Vecchia–Laplace filters that preserve sparsity through forecast and update and scale approximately linearly regardless of temporal evolution sparsity [D] It yields Kalman–Vecchia–Laplace filters that do not preserve sparsity and scale sublinearly with state dimension only when temporal evolution is dense

Embedding the hierarchical Vecchia structure into linear and linearized spatiotemporal state-space models yields Kalman–Vecchia–Laplace filters that preserve sparsity through forecast and update steps and, given sparse temporal evolution, scale approximately _________(linearly/quadratically) with state dimension.

Assertion (A): Embedding the hierarchical Vecchia structure into linear and linearized spatiotemporal state-space models yields Kalman–Vecchia–Laplace filters that preserve sparsity through forecast and update steps and scale approximately linearly with state dimension when temporal evolution is sparse. Reason (R): The resulting filters require approximately quadratic scaling with state dimension under sparse temporal evolution.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): In sequential Bayesian parameter estimation with additive Gaussian observation noise, when the SMCS forward kernel is defined via the Ensemble Kalman filter as a Gaussian centered at the previous particle plus a Kalman-gain–weighted innovation with covariance given by propagated observation noise with small jitter and is paired with a Gaussian approximation to the optimal backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble, under what condition does the resulting tractable sampler have a proposal marginal that approximates the current posterior? [A] Always, regardless of the quality of the previous proposal. [B] Only when the observation noise is heavy-tailed and non-Gaussian. [C] Whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior. [D] Only in exactly linear-Gaussian models with an exact Kalman filter.

In sequential Bayesian parameter estimation with additive Gaussian observation noise, an SMCS forward kernel defined via the Ensemble Kalman filter as a Gaussian centered at the previous particle plus a Kalman-gain–weighted innovation with covariance given by propagated observation noise _________(with small jitter/without jitter), paired with a Gaussian approximation to the optimal backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble, yields a tractable sampler whose proposal marginal approximates the current posterior whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior.

Assertion (A): In sequential Bayesian parameter estimation with additive Gaussian observation noise, defining the SMCS forward kernel via the Ensemble Kalman filter as a Gaussian centered at the previous particle plus a Kalman-gain–weighted innovation with covariance given by propagated observation noise with small jitter and pairing it with a Gaussian approximation to the optimal backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble yields a tractable sampler whose proposal marginal approximates the current posterior whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior. Reason (R): The forward kernel is independent of the previous particle and the backward kernel is exact rather than fitted.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): In an adaptive weight refinement scheme that employs Gaussian surrogate–based approximate weights and activates exact weight computation when an effective-sample-size threshold is crossed, what combined effect is achieved? [A] Increased forward model evaluations with approximate weights retained at refinement steps. [B] Reduced forward model evaluations and preservation of exact importance weights at refinement steps. [C] No change in forward model evaluations and abandonment of exact importance weights at refinement steps. [D] Reduced forward model evaluations only by avoiding any exact weight computation.

Assertion (A): An adaptive weight refinement scheme using inexpensive Gaussian-surrogate approximate weights and an effective-sample-size threshold for triggering exact weight computation reduces forward model evaluations while preserving exact importance weights at refinement steps. Reason (R): Exact weights are never computed, so evaluation costs are minimized.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): In elastic parallel tree search in the cloud, a user-defined target efficiency is maintained by which metric–action pair, and what cross-run cost property does this enforce? [A] Metric: memory bandwidth utilization; Action: static resource capping; Property: constant runtime per run [B] Metric: queue latency; Action: dynamic storage scaling; Property: constant throughput regardless of problem size [C] Metric: workload efficiency—a runtime measure of the fraction of essential computation approximating elastic efficiency; Action: dynamic scaling of processing units to meet the target; Property: constant cost-per-problem-size ratio across runs [D] Metric: wall-clock time; Action: fixed number of compute nodes; Property: decreasing cost with increasing problem size

Maintaining a user-defined target efficiency for elastic parallel tree search in the cloud is accomplished by monitoring wall-clock time rather than workload efficiency and keeping processing units fixed, which yields a constant runtime across runs rather than a constant cost-per-problem-size ratio.

In elastic parallel tree search in the cloud, a user-defined target efficiency is maintained by monitoring workload efficiency and dynamically scaling _________(processing units/storage capacity) to meet this target, which enforces a constant cost-per-problem-size ratio across runs.

Assertion (A): In elastic parallel tree search in the cloud, maintaining a user-defined target efficiency relies on monitoring workload efficiency and dynamically scaling processing units to meet the target, which enforces a constant cost-per-problem-size ratio across runs. Reason (R): Keeping processing units fixed while monitoring queue latency stabilizes throughput independently of problem size.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): In the described setup for session-based job recommendation, what combination of modeling, data, retrieval, and outcomes is correct? [A] Embeddings from classical, denoising, and variational autoencoders trained on interactions and content, used with k-nearest-neighbor retrieval, deliver recommendations with low coverage, high accuracy, and lower novelty than established baselines. [B] Latent session embeddings learned via autoencoders (classical, denoising, variational) from interactions only, retrieved with k-nearest-neighbor, produce recommendations that maintain high coverage, achieve competitive accuracy, and increase system- and session-level novelty, without considering content features. [C] Learning latent session representations with classical, denoising, and variational autoencoders from interaction data alone or combined with content features, and using those embeddings for k-nearest-neighbor retrieval, yields recommendations that maintain high coverage, achieve competitive accuracy, and increase both system- and session-level novelty compared to established baselines. [D] Learning latent representations with denoising autoencoders only from content features and retrieving with approximate nearest neighbors yields recommendations that improve novelty but hurt coverage and accuracy relative to baselines.

Learning latent representations of anonymous job-browsing sessions with classical, denoising, and variational autoencoders trained only on content features and ranked using k-means clustering yields session-based job recommendations that lower coverage, match or exceed accuracy, and diminish both system- and session-level novelty compared with established baselines.

Learning latent representations of anonymous job-browsing sessions with classical, denoising, and variational autoencoders from interaction data alone or combined with content features and using these embeddings for _________(k-nearest-neighbor/linear regression) retrieval yields session-based job recommendations that maintain high coverage, achieve competitive accuracy, and increase system- and session-level novelty relative to established baselines.

Assertion (A): Learning latent representations of anonymous job-browsing sessions via classical, denoising, and variational autoencoders from interaction data alone or combined with content, and using the embeddings for k-nearest-neighbor retrieval, yields session-based job recommendations that maintain high coverage, achieve competitive accuracy, and increase system- and session-level novelty relative to established baselines. Reason (R): The approach relies exclusively on content features and exact-match retrieval, which inherently maximizes novelty while reducing coverage and accuracy.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Under what training data and deployment conditions does a deep encoder–decoder convolutional network achieve automatic people segmentation in surveillance footage, according to the stated claim? [A] It requires auxiliary annotations such as pose or contours and a fixed camera, regardless of the training data. [B] It works only when subjects exhibit constrained motion and the training uses arbitrary synthetic backgrounds. [C] It works when trained on composited green-screen human shapes over site-specific background frames, and it does not require a static camera, constrained subject motion, or auxiliary annotations such as pose or contours. [D] It requires manual pixelwise masks for surveillance frames during training and deployment.

Trained on composited images of green-screen-captured human shapes over site-specific background frames, a deep encoder–decoder convolutional network can automatically segment people in surveillance videos _________(without requiring a static camera/only with a static camera), while not needing constrained subject motion or auxiliary annotations such as pose or contours.

Assertion (A): A deep encoder–decoder convolutional network trained on composited images of green-screen-captured human shapes over site-specific background frames can automatically segment people in surveillance videos without requiring a static camera, constrained subject motion, or auxiliary annotations such as pose or contours. Reason (R): The approach relies on explicit pose and contour annotations during training to learn segmentation and therefore needs a static camera.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): In training scenarios with synthetic composites, which approach improves generalization to real indoor and outdoor images, and for what reason? [A] Guided filtering of real test images to reduce low-frequency lighting differences, yielding sharper boundaries unlike natural transitions. [B] Applying a bilateral filter to synthetic training composites to introduce synthetic textures that differ from natural boundary transitions, harming generalization. [C] Applying a bilateral filter to synthetic training composites to attenuate high-frequency foreground–background edge discrepancies improves generalization to real indoor and outdoor images by better matching natural boundary transitions. [D] Edge detection on synthetic training composites to increase high-frequency boundary contrast, mimicking natural transitions.

Assertion (A): Applying a bilateral filter to synthetic training composites to attenuate high-frequency foreground–background edge discrepancies improves generalization to real indoor and outdoor images. Reason (R): Bilateral filtering primarily increases high-frequency edge contrast at boundaries, amplifying discrepancies between synthetic and natural transitions.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement correctly characterizes the role of inverse-frequency class weighting within cross-entropy loss regarding foreground–background imbalance and foreground pixel classification? [A] Uniform class weighting mitigates the imbalance and improves foreground pixel classification. [B] Inverse-frequency class weighting mitigates the imbalance and improves foreground pixel classification. [C] Frequency-proportional weighting mitigates the imbalance and improves foreground pixel classification. [D] Removing class weights mitigates the imbalance and improves foreground pixel classification.

Using inverse-frequency class weights within cross-entropy loss _________(addresses foreground–background imbalance and enhances foreground pixel classification/exacerbates imbalance and harms foreground pixel classification)
output: addresses foreground–background imbalance and enhances foreground pixel classification

input: Assertion (A): Inverse-frequency weighting of classes in cross-entropy loss mitigates foreground–background imbalance and enhances foreground pixel classification. Reason (R): Giving higher weights to more frequent classes focuses learning on the background and is the source of the improvement in foreground classification.
output: A is True, R is False

input: An architecture with a VGG-16–initialized encoder and a decoder that upsamples via _________(unpooling using stored pooling indices/transposed convolution) enables memory-efficient dense pixel classification suitable for video surveillance streams.

Assertion (A): An architecture with a VGG-16–initialized encoder and a decoder that upsamples via unpooling using stored pooling indices enables memory-efficient dense pixel classification suitable for video surveillance streams. Reason (R): The architecture uses a ResNet-50–initialized encoder and a decoder that upsamples via transposed convolution.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): For multi-step short-term urban traffic flow prediction, particularly under non-recurring disruptions, which configuration yields the reported advantage over traffic-only inputs? [A] Data-level fusion of traffic measurements, weather variables, and geo-specific Twitter signals about traffic events filtered to reliable road-traffic organization accounts within a deep bidirectional LSTM stacked autoencoder, yielding higher accuracy. [B] Feature-level fusion of traffic measurements and unfiltered, generic Twitter data within a shallow feedforward network, yielding similar accuracy. [C] Data-level fusion of traffic measurements and air-quality sensors within a unidirectional GRU, yielding higher accuracy than weather-only inputs. [D] Using traffic measurements alone within a deep bidirectional LSTM stacked autoencoder, with improvements limited to recurring congestion periods.

Data-level fusion of traffic measurements, weather variables, and unfiltered geo-specific Twitter signals within a deep bidirectional LSTM stacked autoencoder does not improve multi-step short-term urban traffic flow prediction accuracy relative to traffic-only approaches and offers no benefit under non-recurring disruptions.

Data-level fusion of traffic measurements, weather variables, and geo-specific Twitter signals about traffic events—filtered to reliable road-traffic organization accounts—within a deep _________(bidirectional/unidirectional) LSTM stacked autoencoder improves multi-step short-term urban traffic flow prediction accuracy relative to traffic-only approaches, particularly under non-recurring disruptions.

Assertion (A): Data-level fusion of traffic measurements, weather variables, and geo-specific Twitter signals about traffic events—filtered to reliable road-traffic organization accounts—within a deep bidirectional LSTM stacked autoencoder improves multi-step short-term urban traffic flow prediction accuracy relative to traffic-only approaches, particularly under non-recurring disruptions. Reason (R): The gains occur only for recurring congestion and not for non-recurring disruptions.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Fast, accurate energy estimation for microcontroller-class edge systems is achieved by pairing OS-independent DVFS in GEM5’s system call emulation mode—triggered by application-issued pseudo-instructions that switch among predefined CPU clock domains—with an architecture-agnostic energy model that decomposes dynamic and leakage power, parameterizes effective capacitance by workload class calibrated from hardware measurements, and accounts for DVFS transition energy, sleep modes, and systematic offset corrections, using only GEM5 instruction-mix and activity statistics. Which option states this correctly? [A] Requires integration with an OS governor and detailed hardware counters, and targets mobile SoCs [B] Uses OS-independent pseudo-instruction-triggered DVFS in GEM5 SE mode with an architecture-agnostic, calibrated, decomposition-based energy model that includes transition energy, sleep modes, and systematic offsets, and relies solely on GEM5 instruction-mix and activity statistics to estimate energy accurately and quickly for microcontroller-class edge systems [C] Relies on OS timers to trigger DVFS and a black-box regression model trained per architecture, excluding sleep and transition effects, to approximate throughput for data-center servers using simulator pipeline traces [D] Employs predefined GPU clock domains and architecture-specific capacitance parameters without hardware calibration, requiring full-system simulation and external power logs to estimate energy for desktop CPUs

An OS-dependent DVFS mechanism in GEM5’s full-system mode, driven by OS schedulers rather than application pseudo-instructions and switching undefined clock domains, paired with an architecture-specific energy model that neither separates dynamic and leakage power nor accounts for DVFS transition energy, sleep modes, or systematic offset corrections, enables fast, accurate energy estimation for microcontroller-class edge systems only when supplemented with OS traces beyond GEM5 instruction-mix and activity statistics.

An OS-independent DVFS capability in GEM5’s system call emulation mode triggered by application-issued pseudo-instructions that switch among predefined CPU clock domains, combined with an _________(architecture-agnostic/architecture-specific) energy model that decomposes dynamic and leakage power and parameterizes effective capacitance by workload class calibrated from hardware measurements—while accounting for DVFS transition energy, sleep modes, and systematic offset corrections—enables fast, accurate energy estimation for microcontroller-class edge systems using only GEM5 instruction-mix and activity statistics.

Assertion (A): An OS-independent DVFS capability in GEM5’s system call emulation mode, triggered by application-issued pseudo-instructions switching among predefined CPU clock domains, combined with an architecture-agnostic energy model that decomposes dynamic and leakage power, parameterizes effective capacitance by workload class calibrated from hardware measurements, and accounts for DVFS transition energy, sleep modes, and systematic offset corrections, enables fast, accurate energy estimation for microcontroller-class edge systems using only GEM5 instruction-mix and activity statistics. Reason (R): The approach relies on OS governors and kernel traces rather than pseudo-instructions and GEM5 statistics to estimate energy.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement correctly characterizes both the composition and comparative performance across datasets in motor imagery EEG classification? [A] A pipeline using discriminative filter bank CSP features and a Gaussian RBF-kernel SVM, optionally preceded by KPCA, achieves comparable but not superior performance across datasets [B] A pipeline using DS-LVQ-weighted discriminative filter bank CSP features but classified with k-nearest neighbor surpasses neural networks only on a single dataset [C] A pipeline using discriminative filter bank CSP features weighted by discriminative sensitive learning vector quantization, classified by a soft-margin SVM with a generalized radial basis function kernel, and optionally preceded by GRBF-based kernel linear discriminant analysis outperforms Gaussian RBF SVMs, KPCA-based pipelines, k-nearest neighbor, and neural networks across datasets [D] A pipeline that replaces DS-LVQ with principal component analysis and uses a linear SVM matches GRBF-based LDA performance but lags behind neural networks across datasets

In motor imagery EEG classification, a pipeline using discriminative filter bank common spatial pattern features weighted by discriminative sensitive learning vector quantization and classified with a _________(soft-margin/hard-margin) support vector machine employing a generalized radial basis function kernel—optionally preceded by GRBF-based kernel linear discriminant analysis—achieves superior accuracy and robustness compared with Gaussian RBF SVMs, KPCA-based pipelines, k-nearest neighbor, and neural networks across datasets.

Assertion (A): In motor imagery EEG classification, a pipeline that uses DS-LVQ-weighted discriminative filter bank common spatial pattern features, classifies with a soft-margin SVM employing a generalized radial basis function kernel, and can be preceded by GRBF-based kernel linear discriminant analysis achieves superior accuracy and robustness across datasets compared with Gaussian RBF SVMs, KPCA-based pipelines, k-nearest neighbor, and neural networks. Reason (R): The performance is due to relying on Gaussian RBF kernels and kernel principal component analysis for representation and classification.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which combination best characterizes outcomes when using Bayesian Hamiltonian Monte Carlo on continuous-state approximations to agent-based financial market models, considering inference rigor, model comparison, mispricing mechanisms, and posterior/identifiability properties? [A] Non-Bayesian estimation, no predictive likelihood-based comparison, superior predictive performance to stochastic volatility benchmarks, and simple, well-identified posteriors. [B] Bayesian HMC with continuous-state approximations, rigorous parameter inference and predictive likelihood-based comparison, mispricing mechanisms with predictive performance comparable to stochastic volatility benchmarks, and complex posteriors with limited parameter identifiability. [C] Approximate Bayesian computation without predictive likelihoods, no reliable parameter inference, mispricing mechanisms lagging stochastic volatility benchmarks, and clear identifiability with simple posteriors. [D] Variational Bayes on discrete-state representations, comparable performance only without mispricing, and convex posteriors with uniquely identified parameters.

Bayesian estimation via Hamiltonian Monte Carlo of continuous-state approximations to agent-based financial market models does not enable predictive likelihood-based comparison, and mechanisms allowing persistent mispricing deliver inferior predictive performance to stochastic volatility benchmarks while yielding simple posterior geometries with strong parameter identifiability.

In Bayesian Hamiltonian Monte Carlo estimation of continuous-state approximations to agent-based financial market models, mechanisms allowing persistent mispricing deliver predictive performance _________(comparable/inferior) to stochastic volatility benchmarks while posteriors exhibit complex geometries and limited parameter identifiability, and the approach enables rigorous parameter inference and predictive likelihood-based comparison.

Assertion (A): Bayesian estimation via Hamiltonian Monte Carlo of continuous-state approximations to agent-based financial market models enables rigorous parameter inference and predictive likelihood-based comparison, with mechanisms allowing persistent mispricing delivering predictive performance comparable to stochastic volatility benchmarks while exhibiting complex posterior geometries and limited parameter identifiability. Reason (R): Allowing persistent mispricing leads to worse predictive performance than stochastic volatility benchmarks and yields simple, well-identified posteriors under this estimation.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Relative to synchronous approaches, what is the effect on pedestrian detection of combining data parallelism with parameter parallelism and using asynchronous parameter updates in a parameter-server architecture? [A] It reduces synchronization overhead and accelerates distributed training [B] It reduces synchronization overhead but slows distributed training [C] It increases synchronization overhead while accelerating training [D] It leaves synchronization overhead unchanged and has no effect on training speed

In pedestrian detection, combining data parallelism with parameter parallelism and using asynchronous parameter updates in a parameter-server architecture increases synchronization overhead and slows distributed training relative to synchronous approaches.

In the context of pedestrian detection, using a parameter-server architecture that combines data parallelism with parameter parallelism _________(reduces/increases) synchronization overhead and accelerates distributed training when updates are asynchronous relative to synchronous approaches.

Assertion (A): Combining data parallelism with parameter parallelism and using asynchronous parameter updates in a parameter-server architecture reduces synchronization overhead and accelerates distributed training for pedestrian detection relative to synchronous approaches. Reason (R): Synchronous approaches eliminate coordination costs by allowing workers to update parameters without waiting for others.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): For distributed deep learning workloads, which statement correctly characterizes OS-level containers (such as LXD) compared with hypervisor-based virtualization regarding resource utilization efficiency? [A] OS-level containers have lower resource utilization efficiency [B] OS-level containers have higher resource utilization efficiency [C] Both approaches have identical resource utilization efficiency [D] The claim applies only to single-node, non-distributed scenarios
When deploying distributed deep learning workloads, hypervisor-based virtualization achieves higher resource utilization efficiency than OS-level containers such as LXD.

For distributed deep learning workloads, compared with hypervisor-based virtualization, OS-level containers like LXD achieve _________(higher resource utilization efficiency/equal resource utilization efficiency).

Assertion (A): Deploying distributed deep learning workloads in OS-level containers such as LXD yields higher resource utilization efficiency than hypervisor-based virtualization. Reason (R): OS-level containers rely on a separate hypervisor that fully emulates hardware for each workload.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): What is the effect of co-locating a parameter server and a slave worker within the same VM on each physical node while avoiding multiple slaves per VM? [A] It increases remote communication and slows overall training performance [B] It has no significant impact on communication or performance [C] It minimizes remote communication and improves overall training performance [D] It only affects inference latency, not training performance

Separating the parameter server and the slave worker into different VMs on each physical node and allowing multiple slave workers per VM minimizes remote communication and improves overall training performance.

Co-locating a parameter server and a slave worker within the _________(same/different) VM on each physical node while avoiding multiple slaves per VM minimizes remote communication and improves overall training performance.

Assertion (A): Co-locating a parameter server and a slave worker within the same VM on each physical node while avoiding multiple slaves per VM minimizes remote communication and improves overall training performance. Reason (R): Running multiple slave workers per VM always enhances locality and thus reduces remote communication.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Regarding scaling training with additional parameter servers or slave workers, which is accurate? [A] Performance always improves until all hardware is utilized, after which it plateaus and is unaffected by parameter size, data distribution, or bandwidth. [B] Performance does not follow a monotonic trend; there exists an optimal configuration shaped by model parameter size, data distribution, and network bandwidth. [C] Performance worsens with added workers but improves with added parameter servers, regardless of model parameter size, data distribution, and bandwidth. [D] Performance improvements are strictly determined by CPU frequency and are unrelated to model parameter size, data distribution, or network bandwidth.
Training performance monotonically improves as parameter servers or slave workers are added, and any optimum is independent of model parameter size, data distribution, and network bandwidth.

The best achievable training performance is _________(configuration-dependent/hardware-fixed), shaped by model parameter size, data distribution, and network bandwidth, rather than increasing monotonically with more parameter servers or slave workers.

Assertion (A): Training performance does not monotonically improve with additional parameter servers or slave workers and instead has an optimum governed by model parameter size, data distribution, and network bandwidth. Reason (R): The optimum is unaffected by model parameter size, data distribution, or network bandwidth.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Select the configuration and outcome that match this scenario: with weekly regional data from Taiwan, the model consumes historical ILI consultation rates together with PM2.5 and, compared with a standard recurrent neural network, attains lower-error short-term ILI forecasts; the forecasts are then combined with WHO five-year outbreak thresholds to enable threshold-based early warning of potential surges. [A] Using weekly regional data from Taiwan, a gated recurrent unit with ILI and NO2 outperforms an LSTM and uses national monthly thresholds for retrospective alerts. [B] Using weekly regional data from Taiwan, an LSTM with ILI and PM2.5 outperforms a standard recurrent neural network and, when paired with WHO five-year outbreak thresholds, enables threshold-based early warning of potential ILI surges. [C] Using weekly regional data from Taiwan, a standard recurrent neural network with ILI only outperforms an LSTM and relies on regional ad hoc thresholds for non-threshold warnings. [D] Using weekly regional data from Taiwan, an LSTM with mobility data matches a standard recurrent neural network and uses WHO ten-year thresholds for non-threshold warnings.

Using daily national data from Taiwan, a standard recurrent neural network that ingests historical influenza-like illness consultation rates and ozone yields lower-error short-term ILI forecasts than an LSTM, and coupling these forecasts with CDC five-year thresholds enables threshold-based early warning of potential ILI surges.

Using weekly regional data from Taiwan, an LSTM that ingests historical influenza-like illness consultation rates and _________(PM2.5/ozone) yields lower-error short-term ILI forecasts than a standard recurrent neural network, and coupling these forecasts with WHO five-year outbreak thresholds enables threshold-based early warning of potential ILI surges.

Assertion (A): Using weekly regional data from Taiwan, an LSTM that ingests historical influenza-like illness consultation rates and PM2.5 yields lower-error short-term ILI forecasts than a standard recurrent neural network, and coupling these forecasts with WHO five-year outbreak thresholds enables threshold-based early warning of potential ILI surges. Reason (R): The approach instead used daily national data and arbitrary monthly thresholds.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): What is asserted about domain-specific word embeddings for mathematics in terms of data source, representational properties, and downstream utility? [A] They can be used without any training corpus and mainly enable clustering by document length while offering no task-specific benefits. [B] When trained on social media text, they capture pragmatic cues and primarily aid sarcasm detection and topic moderation. [C] When trained on mathematical corpora, they capture semantic relationships among mathematical terms and expressions and enable term similarity, analogy search, concept modeling, and query expansion. [D] They require annotated logic graphs instead of corpora and only support theorem proving and type checking.

Math-specific word embeddings trained on general, non-mathematical corpora capture only syntactic patterns and are unsuitable for term similarity, analogy search, concept modeling, or query expansion.

Because they are trained on mathematical corpora, math-specific word embeddings can support tasks such as term similarity, analogy search, concept modeling, and query expansion by _________(capturing semantic relationships among mathematical terms and expressions/counting only document-level term frequencies).
output: capturing semantic relationships among mathematical terms and expressions

input: Assertion (A): Math-specific word embeddings trained on mathematical corpora capture semantic relationships among mathematical terms and expressions and can support term similarity, analogy search, concept modeling, and query expansion. Reason (R): They achieve these capabilities because they are trained exclusively on non-mathematical general-language corpora that omit mathematical expressions.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): When mathematical expressions are represented as streams of tokens, which combination of outcomes is accurate? [A] Enables learning of similarities and ensures reliable identifier–definiens linking due to reduced ambiguity. [B] Fails to enable learning of similarities but is adequate for reliable identifier–definiens linking regardless of context. [C] Enables learning of similarities among mathematical objects but is inadequate for reliably linking identifiers to textual definiens because of ambiguity and context dependence. [D] Has no effect on learning similarities or on linking identifiers to definiens.

Representing mathematical expressions as streams of tokens enables reliable linking of identifiers to textual definiens regardless of ambiguity and context dependence, but does not aid learning of similarities among mathematical objects.

Representing mathematical expressions as streams of tokens enables learning of similarities among mathematical objects but is inadequate for reliably linking identifiers to textual definiens due to _________(ambiguity and context dependence/clear, uniform context).

Assertion (A): Representing mathematical expressions as streams of tokens enables learning of similarities among mathematical objects but is inadequate for reliably linking identifiers to textual definiens. Reason (R): Tokenization fully preserves semantic context for identifiers in expressions.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): In the context of effective machine learning for mathematical information retrieval and semantic extraction, which statement is accurate? [A] Minor issues like hyperparameter tuning are the primary obstacles; standardized datasets and semantic specifications are abundant. [B] Only the lack of public evaluation scripts hinders progress; semantic standards and datasets are otherwise sufficient. [C] Two major obstacles are the lack of precise, canonical semantic specifications for identifiers and functions in MathML/OpenMath and the absence of standardized datasets and benchmarks. [D] Hardware limitations, not data or semantics, constitute the main barrier.

Effective machine learning for mathematical information retrieval and semantic extraction is hindered primarily by hardware constraints, while canonical semantics in MathML/OpenMath and standardized datasets and benchmarks are already well established and thus not obstacles.

Assertion (A): The main barriers to effective machine learning for mathematical information retrieval and semantic extraction are the lack of precise, canonical semantic specifications in MathML/OpenMath for identifiers and functions and the absence of standardized datasets and benchmarks. Reason (R): These tasks depend only on string-level pattern matching, so semantic definitions and standardized datasets are unnecessary.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): In evaluating representation choices for mathematical expressions, which is accurate? [A] Comparing equations is best served by per-identifier embeddings, while identifier semantics are recovered by current embeddings without extra structure. [B] Embedding entire equations as single tokens aids equation-to-equation comparison, but inferring the semantics of individual identifiers demands structural grouping and canonicalization not provided by current embedding techniques. [C] Both equation comparison and identifier semantic recovery require only bag-of-identifier embeddings. [D] Equation comparison and identifier semantic recovery are equally achievable with current embeddings without additional structuring.

Embedding entire equations as single tokens facilitates comparison of equations, and recovering the semantics of individual identifiers is readily handled by current embedding techniques without structural grouping or canonicalization.

For mathematical expressions, using _________(single-token equation embeddings/identifier-level embeddings) helps compare equations, while understanding the semantics of individual identifiers necessitates additional structural grouping and canonicalization beyond current embedding techniques.

Assertion (A): Embedding entire equations as single tokens facilitates equation comparison, whereas recovering the semantics of individual identifiers requires structural grouping and canonicalization beyond current embedding techniques. Reason (R): Current embedding techniques already perform complete canonicalization of identifier semantics, eliminating any need for additional structural grouping.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): For Word2vec-style embeddings trained on math texts, what property of similarity and its effect on automated end-to-end retrieval is plausible? [A] Symmetric similarity leading to a neutral effect on reliability. [B] Non-symmetric similarity leading to improved reliability. [C] Non-symmetric similarity leading to limited reliability. [D] Asymmetric training leading to guaranteed reliability.
Word2vec-style embeddings trained on math texts necessarily maintain symmetric similarity, so they do not limit reliability for automated end-to-end retrieval scenarios.
Because of their potential non-symmetric similarity behavior on math texts, Word2vec-style embeddings can _________(limit/improve) reliability for automated end-to-end retrieval scenarios.

Assertion (A): Word2vec-style embeddings trained on math texts can exhibit non-symmetric similarity behavior that limits reliability for automated end-to-end retrieval scenarios. Reason (R): Similarity measures in such embeddings are guaranteed to be symmetric, ensuring consistent retrieval.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which condition leads to visual attention being directed to corresponding visual attributes along with higher physiological arousal and improved perceived quality of experience compared to video-only presentation? [A] Incongruent olfactory and auditory cues paired with unrelated haptic feedback. [B] Congruent olfactory and auditory cues mapped from visual features with derived haptic feedback in mulsemedia. [C] Video-only playback without any additional sensory cues. [D] Congruent auditory cues alone without olfactory or haptic components.

In mulsemedia, mapping visual features to congruent olfactory and auditory cues with derived haptic feedback reduces physiological arousal, worsens perceived quality of experience, and fails to steer attention toward corresponding visual attributes compared to video-only presentation.

In mulsemedia, aligning olfactory and auditory cues congruently with visual features and adding derived haptic feedback, relative to video-only presentation, _________(directs attention to the corresponding visual attributes and results in higher physiological arousal with better perceived quality of experience/diffuses attention and lowers physiological arousal with worse perceived quality of experience).
output: directs attention to the corresponding visual attributes and results in higher physiological arousal with better perceived quality of experience

input: Assertion (A): Mapping visual features to congruent olfactory and auditory cues with derived haptic feedback in mulsemedia steers visual attention toward the corresponding visual attributes and produces higher physiological arousal with improved perceived quality of experience compared to video-only presentation. Reason (R): The same outcomes are obtained with video-only presentation without any additional sensory cues.
output: A is True, R is False

input: When audiovisual, olfactory, and haptic modalities are combined in mulsemedia, overall Quality of Experience is _________(not significantly higher/significantly higher) with cross-modally matched odours than with a single control scent.

Assertion (A): In mulsemedia experiences combining audiovisual, olfactory, and haptic stimuli, cross-modally matched odours do not significantly enhance overall Quality of Experience compared with a single control scent. Reason (R): Cross-modally matched odours significantly enhance overall Quality of Experience compared with a single control scent in such experiences.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): In cross-modal olfactory-haptic mulsemedia settings, significant modulation of multiple Quality of Experience facets—perceived relevance, distraction, annoyance, and enjoyment—is attributable to which individual differences? [A] Age, gender, education level, and olfactory sensitivity [B] Only olfactory sensitivity [C] Handedness, income, and prior device ownership [D] Age and gender only

In cross-modal olfactory-haptic mulsemedia settings, individual differences in age, gender, education level, and olfactory sensitivity are unrelated to perceived relevance, distraction, annoyance, and enjoyment as Quality of Experience facets.

Multiple _________(Quality of Experience facets/task performance metrics), including perceived relevance, distraction, annoyance, and enjoyment, are significantly modulated by individual differences in age, gender, education level, and olfactory sensitivity in cross-modal olfactory-haptic mulsemedia settings.

Assertion (A): Individual differences in age, gender, education level, and olfactory sensitivity significantly modulate multiple Quality of Experience facets, including perceived relevance, distraction, annoyance, and enjoyment, in cross-modal olfactory-haptic mulsemedia settings. Reason (R): These effects are non-significant and apply only to unimodal visual tasks.
output: A is True, R is False

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Identify the configuration and purpose of a gamified attacker/defender meta model that integrates architectural layers with security standards. [A] It fuses network, host, and user layers with MITRE ATT&CK and ISO/IEC 27001 to correlate hypothetical threats with training games [B] It merges service, information, and event layers with CAPEC and CVSS to convert vulnerabilities into maturity scores [C] It integrates service, information, and event layers with STIX, CAPEC, CVE/CWE, and NIST SP 800-53 to map concrete attack patterns and observable events to countermeasures for cyber risk assessment and education [D] It links business and data layers with CWE and NIST RMF to benchmark risk appetites without event analysis

A gamified attacker/defender meta model uses MITRE ATT&CK instead of STIX, CAPEC, CVE/CWE and omits NIST SP 800-53 controls while focusing solely on entertainment rather than cyber risk assessment and education.

Assertion (A): A gamified attacker/defender meta model integrates service, information, and event layers with standardized knowledge bases (STIX, CAPEC, CVE/CWE) and NIST SP 800-53 controls to map concrete attack patterns and observable events to corresponding countermeasures for cyber risk assessment and education. Reason (R): It relies on proprietary, nonstandard taxonomies and omits control frameworks to maintain flexibility.
output: A is True, R is False
